varieties.name: Dwarf Black Mulberry (Morus nigra 'Dwarf')
varieties.traits: Compact tree 2-3m, low chill requirement ideal for subtropics
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific bearer, multiple crops per year with pruning

varieties.name: Pakistan Mulberry (Morus macroura 'Pakistan')
varieties.origin: Islamabad, Pakistan; selected for subtropical humid climates
varieties.traits: Fast-growing deciduous tree 9-15m, elongated fruit 7-10cm, drought-tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, continuous harvest throughout season, fruiting 2-3 years after planting
varieties.availability: Multiple Australian nurseries including Ladybird Nursery, Daley's Fruit, Chestnut Hill

varieties.name: Red Shahtoot (Morus macroura 'Red Shahtoot')
varieties.traits: Compact growing habit, smaller than standard Pakistan, fruits can reach 10cm length
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy production, suited for subtropical areas

varieties.name: King White Shahtoot (Morus macroura 'King White' / 'Saharanpur')
varieties.traits: Vigorous tree, fruit 6-10cm long, pale yellow-white when ripe, non-staining

varieties.name: Ichikikijiro Dwarf Mulberry
varieties.traits: True dwarf variety, single flush of growth in spring contributing to small stature, suited to backyards
varieties.yield_notes: Good commercial variety despite dwarf size, early fruiting as early as March in subtropics

varieties.name: Lena Mulberry
varieties.traits: Large vigorous shade tree, can be kept contained with annual pruning
varieties.yield_notes: Should produce second crop in late summer if pruned in late spring, fruit on new growth

varieties.name: Black English Mulberry (Morus nigra 'Black English')
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree, considered highest quality by many, prolific over short season
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific fruit over concentrated period rather than extended season

varieties.name: Hicks Fancy (Morus nigra 'Hicks Fancy')
varieties.origin: Selection for cooler areas but adaptable
varieties.traits: Black mulberry variety, fruiting for many months when established

varieties.name: White Mulberry (Morus alba)
varieties.origin: Native to China, Korea, Japan; cultivated for centuries for silk production
varieties.traits: Deciduous tree up to 12m, very adaptable, fast-growing

varieties.name: Black Mulberry (Morus nigra)
varieties.origin: Native to Western Asia and Middle East, cultivated for thousands of years
varieties.traits: Deep purple-black fruit, highest quality flavor according to many, slow-growing

varieties.name: Shangri-La Mulberry (Morus alba x rubra 'Shangri-La')
varieties.origin: Naples, Florida; bred for Deep South subtropical/tropical conditions
varieties.traits: Medium 12-20ft tree, very large heart-shaped deep green leaves, fast-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, bears at 2-3 years, starts early in season

varieties.name: Illinois Everbearing (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.origin: Illinois, USA 1947; discovered as natural hybrid
varieties.traits: Large tree 20-30ft, fast-growing 3-5ft/year, very hardy and widely adapted
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant, extended season July-September, continuous ripening
varieties.availability: International mail order, Australian specialty importers

varieties.name: Silk Hope (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.traits: Similar to Illinois Everbearing but resistant to popcorn disease, vigorous

varieties.name: Wellington (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.origin: Geneva, New York; New York State Fruit Testing Center
varieties.traits: Heavy producer, good quality fruit, vigorous growth

varieties.name: Oscar (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Early ripening, compact growth possible with pruning

varieties.name: Beautiful Day (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.traits: Pure white fruit, non-staining advantage

varieties.name: Gerardi Dwarf (Morus alba x rubra 'Gerardi Dwarf')
varieties.traits: True natural dwarf 6-8ft mature height, short internodes (1 inch vs 5-6 inch), slow-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Good production for size, early ripening
varieties.availability: Edible Landscaping, Cricket Hill Garden USA, specialty importers

varieties.name: Kokuso / Kokusa (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Early ripening, seedless fruit, compact growth

varieties.name: Early Bird (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.traits: Earliest ripening of all varieties, compact

varieties.name: David Smith (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Compact super hardy, fast-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Incredibly heavy production over very long season, multiple crops

varieties.name: Pendula Weeping Mulberry (Morus alba 'Pendula')
varieties.origin: Weeping form selection of White Mulberry
varieties.traits: Small weeping tree, cascading branches, ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, mainly grown for ornamental form

varieties.name: Nikita White (Morus alba 'Nikita White')
varieties.traits: Naturally compact growth habit, early-ripening

varieties.name: Persian White Mulberry (Morus nigra 'White Persian')
varieties.traits: Usually trained as low hedge or tall shrub

varieties.name: Ivory White Mulberry (Morus 'Ivory')
varieties.traits: Stunning deciduous tree, white fruit emphasis

varieties.name: Sweet Lavender (Morus alba 'Sweet Lavender')
varieties.traits: Unique lavender/white colored fruit

varieties.name: Cooke's Mulberry (Morus)
varieties.traits: City environment tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: Good production late spring to mid-summer

varieties.name: Tea's Mulberry (Morus)
varieties.traits: City environment tolerant

varieties.name: Big White Mulberry (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Very heavy producer, early ripening

varieties.name: El Dorado White (Morus alba 'El Dorado')
varieties.traits: Produces one of largest white mulberries

varieties.name: Tehama (Morus alba 'Tehama')
varieties.traits: Warm climate selection

varieties.name: Northrop (Morus)
varieties.traits: Exceptionally hardy cultivar, original tree survived -50F, smaller fruited

varieties.name: Contorted Mulberry (Morus bombycis 'Unryu')
varieties.traits: Very rare unique variety 6-8ft height, gnarled twisted zigzag branches, ornamental

varieties.name: Native Mulberry (Pipturus argenteus)
varieties.origin: Native to subtropical Queensland rainforest margins
varieties.traits: Fast-growing shrub or small tree 3-8m, non-stinging nettle family, butterfly host plant
varieties.availability: Tucker Bush nursery, native plant specialists

varieties.name: Issai / Dwarf Everbearing
varieties.traits: Compact 2-3m, very easy to root, prolific fruiting on current year's wood
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy spring production, pumps out fruit, multiple crops per year
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries specializing in dwarf varieties

varieties.name: Persian Mulberry (Morus nigra - local Orleans California strain)
varieties.traits: Densely foliated tree 25-30ft tall
varieties.availability: Specialty California nurseries, potential for Brisbane import

varieties.name: White Fruiting (Morus alba 'White Fruiting')
varieties.traits: Medium size tree, spreading and productive

varieties.name: Tice (Morus)
varieties.traits: Suitable for warm climates

varieties.name: White Dove (Morus)
varieties.traits: White fruit variety

varieties.name: Black Beauty (potentially mislabeled, likely Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Dark fruit, commonly available

varieties.name: Hachijo (Morus indica var. kagayamae)
varieties.traits: Island selection, yellow autumn color

varieties.name: Mysore Local / Naati Kaddi (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Karnataka/South India, traditional variety
varieties.traits: Popular in Mysore province historically, suitable for both rainfed and irrigated

varieties.name: M-5 / Kanva-2 (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: Open pollinated hybrid from Mysore local variety, India
varieties.traits: Hybrid of traditional Mysore local, suitable for rainfed and irrigated

varieties.name: S-30 (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: Developed by Central Sericultural Research and Training Institute (CSRTI), Mysore
varieties.traits: Open bush type, simple vertical branches, pale green unlobed ovate smooth succulent leaves

varieties.name: S-34 (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: CSRTI Mysore for rainfed black cotton soils
varieties.traits: Deep extensive root system, grows well under moisture stress conditions
varieties.yield_notes: 17 tonnes/ha/year in black cotton soils under rainfed

varieties.name: S-13 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Fast growing variety with straight branches, unlobed leaves, high moisture content (76%)

varieties.name: S-36 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Open bush type, pale green unlobed ovate smooth coriaceous leaves

varieties.name: S-54 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Developed for tropical states

varieties.name: V-1 / Victory-1 (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: CSRTI Mysore - highest yielding variety developed
varieties.traits: Thick green glossy large unlobed leaves, resistant to leaf spot
varieties.yield_notes: Over 60,000 kg/ha/year - highest of all varieties

varieties.name: MR-2 (Mildew Resistant Variety-2) (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Resistant to powdery mildew disease, popular in plains of Tamil Nadu, susceptible to thrips

varieties.name: RFS 135 and RFS 175 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Found under rainfed conditions, adapted to dry periods

varieties.name: S-3 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Fast growing variety with straight branches, unlobed leaves, high moisture content (76%)

varieties.name: Vishwa / Viswa (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: Developed by Karnataka State Sericulture Research Institute from Dehra Dun collection
varieties.traits: Developed by selection method, adapted to tropical conditions
varieties.yield_notes: 35-40 thousand kg/ha/year under irrigated with regular package of practices

varieties.name: Vishala (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Fast growing variety, six harvests per year possible (60 days/crop), 90% rooting capacity, leaf moisture 76%, moisture retention 85% after 6 hours

varieties.name: Suvarna-2 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Developed by hybridization, 80% rooting capacity, leaf moisture 76-78%, moisture retention 92% after 6 hours

varieties.name: Suvarna-3 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Similar to Suvarna-2, hybrid development, 80% rooting, leaf moisture 76-78%, moisture retention 92%

varieties.name: TG (Thlaghattapura) (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Improved mulberry genotype

varieties.name: S1635 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Promising for dry zone but adaptable

varieties.name: S-162 (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: Central Silk Board high yielding variety
varieties.traits: Suited for Punjab, J&K, U.P. and W. Bengal but adaptable

varieties.name: S-519 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Suited for northern states but may adapt

varieties.name: S-633 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Northern state variety

varieties.name: Assam Local / Assamabola (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Assam region, Northeast India
varieties.traits: Traditional cultivated form of Morus indica from Assam

varieties.name: Berhampore Local (Morus indica)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Berhampore region, traditional variety
varieties.traits: Traditional local cultivated form

varieties.name: Dhar Local (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Traditional local form

varieties.name: Sujanpur-1 through Sujanpur-5 (Morus indica)
varieties.traits: Five traditional selections from same region

varieties.name: Thailand Variety (Morus sp.)
varieties.traits: Highest plant height, longest bud length, more leaf yield

varieties.name: Chinese White (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: White mulberry species adapted to various climates

varieties.name: Tr-10 (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Suited for Jammu and Western Himachal Pradesh

varieties.name: Chak Majra (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Suited for Jammu and Western Himachal regions

varieties.name: Morus laevigata (Wild and Cultivated)
varieties.origin: Native to Indus valley to Assam, found wild and cultivated in tropical/subtropical India
varieties.traits: Grown mainly as shade tree in tea and coffee estates of Tamil Nadu and Kerala, also grown as fruit tree
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, dual purpose shade and fruit
varieties.availability: Kerala tea/coffee estate sources, local nurseries

varieties.name: Morus serrata (Himalayan Mulberry)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Uttaranchal, Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir in natural habitats; also found in Nepal
varieties.traits: Sacred mulberry tree at Joshimath is oldest, about 1200 years old. Commonly grown as tree in Himalayas

varieties.name: Pakistan Mulberry (Morus macroura)
varieties.origin: Selected in Islamabad from humid subtropical with dry winters (Cwa) climate
varieties.traits: Very long fruit 3-4 inches, fast-growing, drought-tolerant once established, self-fertile
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, 2-3 years to fruiting, continuous harvest
varieties.availability: International sources, Kerala tropical fruit specialists

varieties.name: Red Shahtoot (Morus macroura)
varieties.traits: Compact variety, fruit can reach 10cm

varieties.name: King White Shahtoot / Saharanpur (Morus macroura)
varieties.traits: Vigorous growth, very long fruit 6-10cm, white/yellow when ripe

varieties.name: Shangri-La (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.origin: Naples, Florida - developed specifically for Deep South subtropical/tropical humid conditions
varieties.traits: 12-20ft tree, very large heart-shaped leaves, fast-growing, early ripening
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, bears 2-3 years, long season
varieties.availability: International tropical specialists, potential Kerala importers

varieties.name: Illinois Everbearing (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.origin: Illinois USA 1947, natural hybrid discovered as chance seedling
varieties.traits: Large tree 20-30ft, very hardy, widely adapted, fast 3-5ft/year growth
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant, July-September extended season, continuous ripening
varieties.availability: International sources, may adapt to Kerala with disease management

varieties.name: Silk Hope (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.origin: North Carolina selection by A.J. Bullard
varieties.traits: Similar to Illinois but RESISTANT to popcorn disease, vigorous growth

varieties.name: Wellington (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.origin: Geneva, New York at NY State Fruit Testing Center
varieties.traits: Heavy producer, vigorous, good quality

varieties.name: Oscar (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Early ripening, compact possible

varieties.name: Beautiful Day (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.traits: White fruit tree, non-staining

varieties.name: Dwarf Everbearing / Issai (Morus)
varieties.traits: Very compact 2-3m, fruits on current year's wood, insanely easy to root
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy spring production, multiple crops possible
varieties.availability: Tropical specialists, can be easily propagated

varieties.name: Early Bird (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.traits: Earliest ripening of all mulberry varieties, compact

varieties.name: Kokuso / Kokusa (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Early ripening, seedless

varieties.name: Gerardi Dwarf (Morus alba x rubra)
varieties.origin: Illinois, Gerardi Nursery - true natural dwarf
varieties.traits: True dwarf 6-8ft mature, short internodes, very slow-growing

varieties.name: David Smith (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Compact, super hardy, fast-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Incredibly heavy over very long season, everbearing

varieties.name: Nikita White (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Naturally compact, early-ripening

varieties.name: Big White (Morus alba)
varieties.traits: Very heavy producer, early

varieties.name: Morus alba (General White Mulberry species)
varieties.origin: Native to China, Korea, Japan; cultivated for over 4000 years for silk

varieties.name: Morus indica (General Indian Mulberry species)
varieties.origin: INDIGENOUS to India - native to Indian subcontinent, found Kashmir to Tamil Nadu
varieties.traits: Most Indian varieties belong to this species. Widely distributed across India. Grows as bushes in many areas. Monoecious with male and female flowers on same tree.
